creator: markd@Kermodei.COM
text:
Using xorg 7.2 with i810 driver, or xorg 7.3 with i810 or intel drives and DRI enabled will either lock up the X server, or more frequently lock up the entire system. This often leaves video card in wedged state, requiring power cycling.

This is reliably reproduced using blender 2.44.1 (an OpenGL application). Often all that is required is moving the cursor in and out of the Blender window.  Occasionally, more interaction with blender is required.  Disabling DRI prevents this from occurring.  No error messages are received, the display simply locks up, often with the cursor disappearing.

xserver log included, kernel reports:

agp0: <Intel 82830M (830M GMCH) SVGA controller> mem 0xe0000000-0xe7ffffff,0xd0000000-0xd007ffff irq 11 at device 2.0 on pci0
agp0: detected 8060k stolen memory
agp0: aperture size is 128M

Will assist in any debugging of the problem, but lack expertise to debug without
guidance.
